answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: In Bubble Sort, the largest element moves to the end in each pass. That is why the right side gets sorted first.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Selection Sort always picks the minimum element and places it at the front. The left side becomes sorted.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Insertion Sort builds a sorted portion on the left side. It is very efficient when the array is already nearly sorted.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: This is Bubble Sort because the largest element keeps moving to the end in each pass.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Bubble Sort performs the most swaps. Insertion Sort is best for nearly sorted arrays. Selection Sort always performs the same number of comparisons.
